title: Visually add and subtract fractions
description: Practice adding and subtracting fractions that have different denominators. Problems have fraction diagrams.

hints: To add fractions, they need to have the same denominator.
Let's rewrite $\dfrac{3}{4}$ with a denominator of $8$.

To rewrite $\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{4}}$ with a denominator of $8$, we need to figure out what to multiply our current denominator by.

$\purpleD4\times {}? = 8$

We need to multiply the numerator and denominator by $2$.

$\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{4}}=\dfrac{\purpleD{3} \times 2}{\purpleD{4} \times 2} = {\dfrac\purpleD{6}\purpleD{8}}$


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A tape diagram with 2 tapes of equal length. The tape on the top is divided into 4 equal pieces. Each part is labeled one-fourth. The first three parts are shaded. The tape diagram on the bottom is divided into eight equal pieces with each piece labeled one-eighth. The first part is shaded.

hints: To add fractions, they need to have the same denominator.

hints: To add fractions, they need to have the same denominator.

hints: To add fractions, they need to have the same denominator.

Let's look at the multiples of each denominator and see which multiples they have in common.

Denominator | Multiples
:-: | :-:
$\blueD{5}$ | $5, {10}, \greenD{15}$
$\maroonD{3}$ | $3, {6}, 9, 12, \greenD{15}$

The least common denominator is $\greenD{15}$.

To rewrite $\blueD{\dfrac{2}{5}}$ with a denominator of $15$, we need to figure out what to multiply our current denominator by.

$\blueD5\times {}? = 15$

We need to multiply the numerator and denominator by $3$.

$\blueD{\dfrac{2}{5}}=\dfrac{\blueD{2} \times 3}{\blueD{5} \times 3} = {\dfrac\blueD{6}\blueD{15}}$



[[☃ image 1]]

To rewrite $\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{3}}$ with a denominator of $15$, we need to figure out what to multiply our current denominator by.

$\maroonD3\times {}? = 15$

We need to multiply the numerator and denominator by $5$.

$\maroonD{\dfrac{1}{3}}=\dfrac{\maroonD{1} \times 5}{\maroonD{3} \times 5} = {\dfrac\maroonD{5}\maroonD{15}}$
